formulas.formula:
However, since the chapter on "Location" in CBSE Class 11 Geography - India does not involve mathematical formulas, I'll provide a list of key statements and laws related to the topic in the given format.

```json
[
  {
    "name": "Vernier Scale Principle",
    "formula": "",
    "variables": "Not applicable",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "A vernier scale is a small linear scale graduated on a movable part, used for measuring small differences in length or for reading the values of a main scale. It is used in instruments like the sextant and theodolite."
  },
  {
    "name": "Latitude and Longitude Grid System",
    "formula": "",
    "variables": "Not applicable",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "The latitude and longitude grid system is a method of measuring the position of a place on the Earth's surface using lines of latitude (0° to 90°) and lines of longitude (0° to 180°)."
  },
  {
    "name": "Time Zone",
    "formula": "",
    "variables": "Not applicable",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Time zones are regions on Earth that follow the same standard time, based on the mean solar time at a specific meridian."
  },
  {
    "name": "Location and Accessibility",
    "formula": "",
    "variables": "Not applicable",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "The location of a place is determined by its latitude, longitude, and altitude. Accessibility refers to the ease with which a place can be reached."
  },
  {
    "name": "Isobars",
    "formula": "",
    "variables": "Not applicable",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Isobars are lines on a map that connect points of equal atmospheric pressure."
  },
  {
    "name": "Isohyets",
    "formula": "",
    "variables": "Not applicable",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Isohyets are lines on a map that connect points of equal precipitation."
  },
  {
    "name": "Isotherms",
    "formula": "",
    "variables": "Not applicable",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Isotherms are lines on a map that connect points of equal temperature."
  }
]
```

Note: Since the chapter on "Location" in CBSE Class 11 Geography - India does not involve mathematical formulas, the above list only includes key statements and laws related to the topic.
